name: Learning Review
description: Registers Learning Review as a first-class Delivery OS lifecycle hook (peer to VERIFY and Founder Review). A 3-level trigger policy decides whether experience is captured continuously (L0), checkpointed lightly (L1), or run through a full multi-specialist retro (L2) — fired by the classifier learning-trigger.mjs on the heavy triggers (incident · ADR/board · new-capability · founder-epic · milestone · census-candidate · N-merge backstop>30), default no-review. Every approved learning is auto-classified by learning-classify.mjs into the right asset of the 10-way taxonomy and inherited via the manifest; an L2 review emits a Founder Learning Package. Eval is FAIL-OPEN (an L0/L1 trigger never blocks); the review decision is CLOSE-DEFAULT (no review unless a trigger fires). This feeds the existing §14 promotion machinery — it does not relax the promotion bar (observed failure OR second consumer) or the §11 panel economics.
ratified: 2026-06-25 (founder-ratified; runs in SHADOW classify+log — turning on gating is a separate founder decision after calibration)

triggerPolicy.evalPosture: fail-open — an L0/L1 classification NEVER blocks a push, a commit, or a goal; only the heavy-trigger L2 path schedules the full review
triggerPolicy.completenessGuarantee: the N-merge backstop (§14, default 30) caps learning debt at 30 commits — the recorded 175-overdue retro becomes structurally impossible

levels.name: continuous capture
levels.fires: every slice — append-only signal capture (capabilities/signals.jsonl via file-lesson.mjs + the gates); no review, no block

levels.name: lightweight checkpoint
levels.fires: phase-end / routine slice boundary — a fast bump-or-declare-no-learning checkpoint; non-blocking

levels.name: full multi-specialist review
levels.fires: any HEAVY trigger — incident · ADR/board decision · new-capability · founder-epic · milestone · census-candidate · the §14 N-merge backstop (>30 commits since the last docs/feedback artifact)
levels.runs: the learning-review skill + a scaled §11 multi-lens panel (compose principle-11's lens machinery) + learning-classify.mjs + emits the Founder Learning Package

triggerPolicy.autoDecisionRule: learning_expected = (level == L1) OR (level == L2); default no review. Every APPROVED learning is auto-classified into exactly the right asset (the 10-way taxonomy) and inherited via os-foundation.manifest.json — never assumed a skill (§14).

output.level: the classified trigger level (L0 / L1 / L2) + learning_expected boolean
output.assets: for an approved learning, the learning-classify.mjs taxonomy bucket(s) (the 10-way asset map) the lesson routes into
output.package: on L2 — docs/learning/LEARNING-<event>.md (the Founder Learning Package) emitted by founder-learning-package.mjs

invoke.sideEffect: write (L2 only — emits the Founder Learning Package + appends classified assets; L0/L1 are read-or-append-only and never block)

observability.outcome: the classified level + learning_expected + the chosen asset bucket(s); on L2, the emitted package path; structured --json report

assets.taxonomy: the 10-way learning taxonomy — each approved lesson routes to exactly one asset (storing it in the wrong artifact is itself a defect, §14): hook · template · doctrine-line · skill · agent-change · lint · process-adjustment · capability-ledger-row · ADR/decision · no-framework-change
assets.inheritance: an approved framework-level asset propagates via os-foundation.manifest.json (tools[]/skills[]/workflows[]) so every future repo inherits it — the inheritance half of §14 is already mechanical

rollout.mode: SHADOW — classify + log before gating (the D3/ADR-003 SHADOW precedent). learning-trigger.mjs classifies and records the level + would-fire decision without scheduling a blocking review, until the trigger calibration is observed on real work.
rollout.promotionBarUnchanged: Learning Review FEEDS the §14 promotion machinery; the promotion bar (observed failure OR second consumer pulling), close-default, the §11 scaled panel, and the no-backflow lint are all UNCHANGED. This registers a hook; it does not relax the bar.
